LAW & MOTION, DEPT. 302, AS TO THE FEB-05-2024 HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF ELISA RECODER'S MOTION FOR DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF'S UNOPPOSED "MOTION FOR DISMISSAL WITHOUT PREJUDICE" IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR.; CLERK: V. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Dismissal Without Prejudice
Off Calendar Feb-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ELISA RECODER's Motion For Dismissal Without Prejudice.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion for dismissal without prejudice" is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported (302/RBU)
